options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Stage Digests
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Stage Digests: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 9465 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile wire and decoded budgets, coding chains, media, charsets, partial bodies, and stage digests; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep raw, decoded, tree, main-content, record, and rendered digests separate with algorithm and version.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Stage Digests.
